title: Whitelabel Strategy
html:
Rankett's GTM lever — sell AI search optimization to agencies, not direct-to-brand, with the agency's logo on every artifact (audit tool, email templates, contracts, fulfillment-bot). The single most-impactful strategic insight in the post-AEO arc. Pairs with 60 day activation rule, ghost white label (the deeper architectural commitment), and pooled data network effect (the moat the white-label posture enables).

TL;DR

  • Sell to / through agencies, not end-clients. Agencies pay Rankett $998/seat; charge their clients $3K–$6K/mo; keep the spread.
  • The white-label posture is architecturally deep — the agency's brand on every touchpoint: audit tool, email templates, contracts, exportable reports, even the fulfillment-bot interface.
  • Born from the AEO Agency pain — direct-to-end-client AEO didn't have margin. The 2025 lesson became the 2026 strategy.
  • Filter for which agencies qualify: 5+ existing clients + 60-day activation (60 day activation rule). Productization, not dev-shop.
  • Enables pooled data network effect — Rankett aggregates ranking data across all client agencies, but the agencies see it as their own.

Key facts

  • The $998/seat economic model. 2026 01 13 rankett com / 2026 01 13 rankettcom — agency pays Rankett $998/mo, charges client $3K–6K/mo. Margin lives at the agency layer; Rankett gets recurring SaaS revenue at scale.
  • Logo-everywhere commitment. 2026 01 05 rankett partner program / 2026 01 06 rankett context handoff — every artifact rebrand-ready: audit, email, contract, fulfillment.
  • "Product, not a dev shop." 2026 01 07 rankett meetup prep gdocs — explicit guardrail against the consulting trap.
  • Ghost white label is the architectural variant. Rankett's brand never appears anywhere — clients don't even know Rankett exists. Maximum agency-brand-protection mode.
  • GTM proof in the wild. 2026 03 06 vazoola content examples — agencies are already doing manual versions of this; Rankett productizes the back-end.
  • Lift ATX adjacency. 2025 12 20 how to make lift atx better uses the same logic for fitness studios — same template, different vertical.

Why this works (per the vault)

  1. Agencies have trust + sales velocity Rankett doesn't. Bypass Rankett's cold-start problem.
  2. Agencies need a productized differentiator. AEO is becoming table-stakes; Rankett is the cheap way to add it.
  3. Margin lives at the agency layer. Agencies keep the brand-relationship rent; Rankett harvests SaaS-scale efficiency.
  4. Network effects compound silently. Pooled data across agencies improves Rankett's ranking model without exposing client data.
  5. Recurring revenue + low CAC. One agency = many clients = predictable seat expansion.

Tensions

  • Pricing-vs-broke contradiction (contradiction pricing vs broke query) — Yahir is "strategically broke" while quoting $998/seat. The model only works at scale; early customers are the bridge.
  • Distribution-and-going-to-market (distribution and going to market query) — agency-channel sales has its own learning curve; Yahir has to become an agency-whisperer.
  • Margin echoes the 2019 expensive ≠ margin insight — old idea, finally applied at the right scale.
